summary: The diff implements both REQ-1 and REQ-2 with largely correct approaches. REQ-2 (frontend pre-fill and auto-seed) is fully implemented. REQ-1 has partial coverage: the migration module seeds the permanent admin and the backend main.mo includes the migration hook, but there are concerns about idempotency and the seeding mechanism used (migration vs. init hook). The frontend seeds via inviteAdminUser when no admin exists, which is a separate path from the backend migration seeding. Overall the core requirements appear addressed but with nuances around the backend initialization path.

missing_requirements.requirement: The credential is seeded during canister initialization so it persists across upgrades (idempotent init/upgrade hook on the backend).
missing_requirements.reason: The backend seeding is done via a migration module (Migration.run) that only seeds when adminUsers.isEmpty(). If the permanent admin entry is removed at runtime, a subsequent upgrade would not re-seed it because adminUsers would not be empty. There is no standalone system func init or system func postupgrade that unconditionally ensures the permanent admin exists regardless of canister state. The idempotency guarantee (always exists regardless of canister state) is not fully satisfied.

hallucinated_features.description: The migration module introduces a schema migration from an old AdminInvitationEntry type with non-optional invitedBy Principal to a new type with optional invitedBy, which was not requested.
hallucinated_features.reason: The build request only asked for seeding a permanent admin credential. The migration module also performs a data-model migration converting existing admin records from a non-optional to optional invitedBy field, which is an unrequested schema change.
